who are you: I am Jorkle, your AI assistant
what is python: Python is a high-level programming language
what is java: Java is a high-level, class-based, object-oriented programming language
what is photosynthesis: Photosynthesis is the process by which green plants use sunlight to synthesize foods from carbon dioxide and water
what is gravity: Gravity is a force by which a planet or other body draws objects toward its center
what is electricity: Electricity is the set of physical phenomena associated with the presence and motion of electric charge
what is an atom: An atom is the smallest unit of ordinary matter that forms a chemical element
what is a molecule: A molecule is a group of atoms bonded together
what is water: Water is a transparent, tasteless, odorless, and nearly colorless chemical substance
what is earth: Earth is the third planet from the Sun
what is world war ii: World War II was a global war that lasted from 1939 to 1945
what is world war i: World War I was a global war originating in Europe that lasted from 1914 to 1918
who was abraham lincoln: Abraham Lincoln was the 16th president of the United States
who was george washington: George Washington was the first president of the United States
who was nelson mandela: Nelson Mandela was a South African anti-apartheid revolutionary and political leader
what is dna: DNA is the hereditary material in almost all living organisms
what is rna: RNA is a molecule essential in various biological roles including coding, decoding, regulation, and expression of genes
what is a virus: A virus is a small infectious agent that replicates only inside the living cells of an organism
what causes rain: Rain is caused by condensed water vapor in the atmosphere falling under gravity
what is the longest river: The Nile is commonly considered the longest river
what is a computer: A computer is a machine that can be instructed to carry out sequences of arithmetic or logical operations automatically
what is the internet: The internet is a global network of computers that communicate via standard protocols
what is email: Email is a method of exchanging messages between people using electronic devices
what is ai: AI stands for artificial intelligence, the simulation of human intelligence in machines
what is machine learning: Machine learning is a subset of AI that gives computers the ability to learn from data
what is deep learning: Deep learning is a subset of machine learning using neural networks with many layers
what is natural language processing: NLP is a field of AI that gives computers the ability to understand and generate human language
what is a database: A database is an organized collection of structured information or data
what is sql: SQL is a standard language for relational database management systems
what is json: JSON is a lightweight data-interchange format
what is xml: XML is a markup language that defines a set of rules for encoding documents
what is an api: An API is a set of rules that allows programs to talk to each other
what is cloud computing: Cloud computing is delivering computing services over the internet
what is linux: Linux is an open-source Unix-like operating system kernel
what is windows: Windows is a group of several proprietary graphical operating system families developed by Microsoft
what is macos: macOS is a series of proprietary graphical operating systems developed by Apple
what is github: GitHub is a provider of Internet hosting for software development and version control using Git
what is git: Git is a distributed version-control system for tracking changes in source code
what is encryption: Encryption is the process of encoding information so that only authorized parties can access it
what is ssl: SSL is a standard security protocol for establishing encrypted links
what is tls: TLS is a cryptographic protocol designed to provide communications security
what are the colors of the rainbow: Red, Orange, Yellow, Green, Blue, Indigo, Violet
what is salt: Salt is a mineral composed primarily of sodium chloride
what is sugar: Sugar commonly refers to sucrose, a carbohydrate
what is a calorie: A calorie is a unit of energy
what is the definition of success: Success is the accomplishment of an aim or purpose
what is love: Love is a complex set of emotions and behaviors characterized by intimacy, passion, and commitment
what is democracy: Democracy is a system of government by the whole population or eligible members of a state
what is a republic: A republic is a form of government in which the country is considered a public matter
what is socialism: Socialism is a political and economic theory of social organization advocating community ownership
what is capitalism: Capitalism is an economic system in which private individuals own and control property
what is communism: Communism is a political and economic ideology aiming for a classless society
what is inflation: Inflation is the rate at which the general level of prices for goods and services is rising
what is deflation: Deflation is a decrease in the general price level of goods and services
what is unemployment: Unemployment is the situation where people capable of working are unable to find work
what is a dollar: A dollar is a unit of currency used by several countries, notably the United States
what is a euro: The euro is the official currency of the eurozone
what is bitcoin: Bitcoin is a decentralized digital currency
what is blockchain: A blockchain is a distributed ledger technology for recording transactions
what is a startup: A startup is a young company founded to develop a unique product or service
what is entrepreneurship: Entrepreneurship is the process of designing, launching, and running a new business
what is marketing: Marketing is the business process of creating relationships with and satisfying customers
what is sales: Sales is the activity of selling products or services
what is finance: Finance is the study of money management
what is investing: Investing is allocating resources, usually money, with the expectation of generating an income or profit
what is interest: Interest is the cost of borrowing money, typically expressed as a percentage
what is a loan: A loan is money borrowed that is expected to be paid back with interest
what is credit: Credit is the trust which allows one party to provide resources to another party
what is a stock: A stock is a share in the ownership of a company
what is a bond: A bond is a fixed income instrument representing a loan made by an investor to a borrower
what is diversification: Diversification is a risk management technique that mixes a wide variety of investments
what is compound interest: Compound interest is interest calculated on the initial principal and also on accumulated interest
what is time zone: A time zone is a region of the globe that observes a uniform standard time
what is utc: UTC stands for Coordinated Universal Time
what is gmt: GMT stands for Greenwich Mean Time
what is the internet of things: IoT refers to the growing network of connected devices that communicate over the internet
what is 5g: 5G is the fifth generation mobile network technology
what is a sensor: A sensor detects and responds to some type of input from the physical environment
what is robotics: Robotics is the branch of technology that deals with the design and application of robots
what is automation: Automation is the technique of making processes operate automatically
what is virtualization: Virtualization is creating a virtual version of something, such as hardware or operating systems
what is a container: A container packages code and its dependencies so applications run quickly and reliably
what is docker: Docker is a platform for developing, shipping, and running applications in containers
what is kubernetes: Kubernetes is an open-source system for automating deployment, scaling, and management of containerized apps
what is ai ethics: AI ethics is the field concerned with the moral behavior of artificially intelligent systems
what is bias in ai: Bias in AI refers to systematic errors that lead to unfair outcomes
what is supervised learning: Supervised learning is a type of machine learning where models are trained on labeled data
what is unsupervised learning: Unsupervised learning trains models on unlabeled data to find structure
what is reinforcement learning: Reinforcement learning trains agents to take actions to maximize cumulative reward
what is cross validation: Cross validation is a model validation technique for assessing how models generalize to independent data
what is overfitting: Overfitting is when a model learns noise in the training data and performs poorly on new data
what is underfitting: Underfitting is when a model is too simple and cannot capture the underlying pattern
what is precision: Precision is the ratio of true positives to all predicted positives
what is recall: Recall is the ratio of true positives to all actual positives
what is f1 score: F1 score is the harmonic mean of precision and recall
what is accuracy: Accuracy is the ratio of correct predictions to total predictions
what is a confusion matrix: A confusion matrix is a table used to describe the performance of a classification model
what is a neural network: A neural network is a series of algorithms that attempts to recognize underlying relationships in data
what is a neuron: A neuron is a computational unit in a neural network that receives inputs and produces an output
what is a layer: A layer is a collection of neurons in a neural network at the same depth
what is a learning rate: Learning rate controls how much to change the model in response to the estimated error each time the model weights are updated
what is gradient descent: Gradient descent is an optimization algorithm used to minimize a function by iteratively moving in the direction of steepest descent
what is batch size: Batch size is the number of training samples used in one iteration
what is epoch: An epoch is one complete pass through the entire training dataset
what is regularization: Regularization is a technique to prevent overfitting by adding information or constraints to a model
what is dropout: Dropout is a regularization technique that randomly ignores a subset of neurons during training
what is activation function: An activation function introduces non-linearity into a neural network
what is relu: ReLU stands for Rectified Linear Unit, an activation function that outputs the input if positive otherwise zero
what is sigmoid: Sigmoid is an activation function that maps inputs to a value between 0 and 1
what is softmax: Softmax is an activation function that turns a vector of values into probabilities that sum to 1
what is batch normalization: Batch normalization normalizes layer inputs for each mini-batch to stabilize learning
what is transfer learning: Transfer learning uses a pre-trained model on a new but related task
what is fine tuning: Fine tuning is adjusting a pre-trained model on a new dataset
what is a prompt: A prompt is the input given to a language model to guide its output
what is tokenization: Tokenization is splitting text into tokens for processing by models
what is an embedding: An embedding is a numeric representation of objects such as words or sentences in a continuous vector space
what is cosine similarity: Cosine similarity measures the cosine of the angle between two non-zero vectors
what is euclidean distance: Euclidean distance is the straight-line distance between two points in Euclidean space
what is a chatbot: A chatbot is a program that can simulate conversation with humans
what is a virtual assistant: A virtual assistant is a software agent that can perform tasks based on commands or questions
what is speech recognition: Speech recognition converts spoken language into text
what is text to speech: Text to speech converts written text into spoken audio
what is computer vision: Computer vision is a field that enables computers to interpret and process visual information from the world
what is object detection: Object detection identifies and locates objects within images or videos
what is image classification: Image classification assigns labels to images based on their content
what is instance segmentation: Instance segmentation detects and delineates each object instance in an image
what is facial recognition: Facial recognition identifies or verifies a person from a digital image or video frame
what is optical character recognition: OCR converts different types of documents into editable and searchable data
what is data science: Data science is the discipline of extracting knowledge and insights from data
what is statistics: Statistics is the study of the collection, analysis, interpretation, presentation, and organization of data
what is probability: Probability measures the likelihood of an event occurring
what is a mean: Mean is the average of a set of numbers
what is a median: Median is the middle value in an ordered set of numbers
what is a mode: Mode is the most frequently occurring value in a dataset
what is variance: Variance measures how far a set of numbers are spread out from their average
what is standard deviation: Standard deviation is the square root of the variance
what is correlation: Correlation measures the relationship between two variables
what is causation: Causation indicates that one event is the result of the occurrence of the other event
what is a sample: A sample is a subset of a population used for statistical analysis
what is a population: A population is the complete set of items that are of interest in a statistical study
what is hypothesis testing: Hypothesis testing is a method of making decisions using data
what is a p value: A p-value helps determine the significance of results in hypothesis testing
what is confidence interval: A confidence interval gives an estimated range of values which is likely to include an unknown population parameter
what is linear regression: Linear regression models the relationship between a scalar dependent variable and one or more explanatory variables
what is logistic regression: Logistic regression models the probability of a binary outcome
what is clustering: Clustering groups a set of objects so that objects in the same group are more similar to each other than those in other groups
what is k means: K-means is an iterative clustering algorithm that partitions data into k clusters
what is hierarchical clustering: Hierarchical clustering builds a multilevel hierarchy of clusters
what is anomaly detection: Anomaly detection finds unusual patterns that do not conform to expected behavior
what is recommendation system: A recommendation system suggests items to users based on preferences or behavior
what is collaborative filtering: Collaborative filtering makes predictions about a user's interests by collecting preferences from many users
what is content based recommendation: Content-based recommendation suggests items similar to those a user liked in the past
what is hybrid recommendation: Hybrid recommendation combines collaborative and content-based methods
what is a metric: A metric is a standard of measurement used to assess performance
what is latency: Latency is the time delay between a request and response
what is throughput: Throughput is the rate of processing work or data over time
what is scalability: Scalability is the ability to handle growing amounts of work by adding resources
what is availability: Availability is the proportion of time a system is functional and working
what is reliability: Reliability is the probability that a system performs without failure over a specified period
what is maintainability: Maintainability is how easily a system can be modified to fix faults or add features
what is observability: Observability is how well internal states of a system can be inferred from its outputs
